name: generating-sorbet-inline
description: Generates Sorbet inline type signatures using sig blocks directly in Ruby source files. Triggers when adding Sorbet types, annotating Ruby methods with sig syntax, or generating type signatures for Sorbet-typed projects.
capabilities: Analyzes Ruby classes, modules, methods, and constants
Generates sig blocks with proper parameter and return types
Validates signatures using 'srb tc' type checker
Handles generics, nilable types, and union types
Preserves existing typed sigil levels in files
author: DmitryPogrebnoy
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill providing instructions for generating Sorbet inline type signatures. All static findings are in the reference/ directory which contains example code from the Packwerk gem (Shopify) for learning purposes. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are for MD5 digests used in cache invalidation (non-security use). Shell backtick execution is for running 'srb tc' validation command (legitimate tooling). No credential access or exfiltration patterns exist. The skill explicitly prohibits running project Ruby code.
valueStatement: Ruby projects need type safety for reliable code. This skill adds Sorbet inline type signatures to methods using sig blocks. It analyzes Ruby source files, generates proper type annotations, and validates signatures with the Sorbet type checker.
userTitle: Add Sorbet type signatures to Ruby methods
limitations: Does not execute or modify project Ruby code
Does not generate .rbi files (only inline signatures)
Requires user to provide type hints for ambiguous cases
Does not upgrade file strictness levels without consent

bestPractices: Start with # typed: true and gradually increase strictness
Use specific types like String, Integer instead of T.untyped
Run 'srb tc' after adding signatures to catch errors early
Ask for clarification when type information is ambiguous
antiPatterns: Using T.untyped as a shortcut defeats the purpose of typing
Changing typed sigil levels without understanding impact
Running project Ruby code during signature generation

faq.question: What is Sorbet?
faq.answer: Sorbet is a static type checker for Ruby developed by Stripe. It validates type annotations at compile time.

faq.question: What is the difference between inline types and RBI files?
faq.answer: Inline types use sig blocks directly in Ruby files. RBI files are separate type stub files for external gems.

faq.question: Does this skill run my Ruby code?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly prohibits running project Ruby code. It only analyzes source structure.

faq.question: What does sig { returns(String) } mean?
faq.answer: It declares that the method returns a String value. The sig block defines types for a method.

faq.question: How do I handle unknown types?
faq.answer: Ask the user for clarification. Use T.any for union types and T.nilable for optional values.

faq.question: What typed sigil should I use?
faq.answer: Start with typed: true for gradual adoption. Increase to strict or strong as your codebase matures.
